message:
feat: implement execSync, spawnSync, execFileSync, fork, appendFileSync, accessSync

Users expect the same code they use in Node.js to work in nodemode.
Previously execSync/spawnSync/execFileSync all threw errors, fork() threw,
and appendFileSync/accessSync were missing entirely.

- Add ProcessManager.execSyncBuiltin() for synchronous builtin execution
- execSync/execFileSync return Buffer (Uint8Array) or string with encoding
- spawnSync returns { status, stdout, stderr, pid, output } like Node.js
- fork() runs child JS file via JsRunner.fork()
- appendFileSync reads existing content from SQLite cache and appends
- accessSync throws ENOENT for missing files
- Sync file-reading builtins (cat, grep, head, tail, wc) read from SQLite cache
- 13 new tests proving real Node.js patterns work
